name: Antonelli's Pizza

notes: Antonelli's current restaurant-issued HTML menu and linked June 2025 PDF reconcile to 80 canonical top-level products across 15 food and nonalcoholic-beverage categories.

All 100 frozen rows were reconciled: 61 exact matches, one normalized match, one truncated-label variant, and 37 promoted headings, modifiers, notes, description fragments, or promotional artifacts. The 63 matched products leave 17 current omissions.
The repair restores all 17 omitted products, excludes twelve Beer & Wine presentations, and keeps the four nonalcoholic beverage products as the final category.

The owner-published HTML/PDF menus provide product names and selected ingredient descriptions but no complete allergen matrix or cross-contact disclosure. Seventy-three current products have supported positive signals; seven remain accurately unavailable.

Forty-five matched frozen allergen outcomes differ from the corrected evidence. The repair maps only positive signals supported by current product, category, or ingredient semantics and never treats silence as safety.
Optional cheese, chicken, bacon, sauces, toppings, meatballs, and deluxe additions are removed before base-product signal mapping so their ingredients do not smear onto the base product.
Beer & Wine is a catalog boundary, not allergen evidence; its twelve presentations remain excluded.

The frozen generic extraction promoted 37 structural or promotional rows into products, omitted 17 current products, flattened almost every product into the Pizza category, and published partial descriptions as names.

The reviewed parser traverses only owner-published top-level price-list products under an explicit category allowlist, handles Buffalo Wings and Plain Cheese structural exceptions, rejects helpers/alcohol, and asserts 97 raw rows, two responsive Plain Cheese tables, 80 products, and 15 categories.
The hidden responsive HTML table's stale $12.99 medium Plain Cheese price is rejected in favor of the $13.99 current row corroborated by the visually inspected PDF.

Focused tests cover source shape, stale-table selection, category ordering, helper and alcohol rejection, optional-add-on semantics, every frozen-row disposition, published repair invariants, target-only isolation, and idempotency.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. Confirmed Antonelli's Pizza & Subs at 8212 Gunston Corner Lane, Lorton, VA 22079 and the owner-controlled menu links.
HTTP 200. Retained the 522,359-byte current owner-published HTML menu and reviewed all 97 price-list rows plus its responsive Plain Cheese tables.

HTTP 200. Retained the 417,421-byte two-page restaurant-issued PDF, rendered and visually inspected both pages at original detail, and corroborated the 80-product catalog and $13.99 medium Plain Cheese price.
Targeted review of the owner-published HTML and PDF found item descriptions but no allergen matrix, may-contain statement, shared-equipment disclosure, or cross-contact assurance.

findings.summary: The frozen extraction promoted 37 headings, modifiers, global notes, description fragments, and promotional rows into standalone products.
findings.resolution: Restricted extraction to reviewed top-level product rows under explicit menu categories and added complete artifact disposition tests.

findings.summary: The app omitted 17 current products and flattened all 63 matched products into incorrect generic categories or descriptions.
findings.resolution: Published all 80 canonical current products in their 15 owner-published categories with Beverages last.

findings.summary: Forty-five matched frozen products had missing or overstated positive signals relative to current product/category/ingredient evidence.
findings.resolution: Recomputed supported positive signals, preserved seven unavailable rows, and invented no negative or cross-contact claim.

findings.summary: The HTML retains a stale hidden responsive Plain Cheese row pricing the medium pizza at $12.99 alongside the current $13.99 row.
findings.resolution: Selected the $13.99 row only after the restaurant-issued PDF independently corroborated it, and asserted both HTML candidates in tests.

findings.summary: Optional cheese, chicken, bacon, sauce, topping, meatball, and deluxe text could incorrectly attach its signals to base products.
findings.resolution: Removed optional-addition clauses before positive-signal mapping and added a regression ensuring Grilled Chicken Sub does not inherit optional cheese.

findings.summary: The official menu includes twelve Beer & Wine presentations after its nonalcoholic beverage section.
findings.resolution: Excluded the alcohol presentations while retaining the four current nonalcoholic beverages at the end of the catalog.

repairs.summary: Rebuilt Antonelli's current menu and corrected structural promotion, omissions, category/description associations, stale price selection, allergen semantics, optional-addition smear, and alcohol scope.
